description: Execute Workflow sub-workflow with mode each — build a Process Order sub-workflow and a parent that calls it once per order without wrapping in Loop Over Items.

conversation.text: Build two workflows: (1) a sub-workflow called Process Order starting with an Execute Workflow Trigger that receives a single order and passes it through, and (2) a parent webhook workflow that, when it receives an orders array, calls Process Order once per order — each order handled independently.

outcomeExpectations: A Process Order sub-workflow exists with an Execute Workflow Trigger and is referenced by the parent workflow's Execute Workflow node.
Each order in the webhook payload triggers its own sub-workflow execution (Execute Workflow in per-item / 'each' mode, or equivalent).
The agent did NOT wrap Execute Workflow in Loop Over Items — Execute Workflow with per-item mode handles the iteration.

executionScenarios.name: three-orders-three-runs
executionScenarios.description: Three orders each invoke the Process Order sub-workflow
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives { "orders": [{ "id": "ORD-10", "total": 50 }, { "id": "ORD-11", "total": 75 }, { "id": "ORD-12", "total": 120 }] }. The Process Order sub-workflow completes successfully for each order when invoked.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. The Process Order sub-workflow runs three times — once per order — processing ORD-10, ORD-11, and ORD-12 independently. A workflow that passes all three orders in one sub-workflow batch without per-order invocation FAILS.
